This is a simple but also somewhat complicated fix to pants that are too loose. Having made some headway in loosing weight (Yay!) I am now dealing with jeans that no longer fit. I can wear a belt, but I don't always want that option.

These offer a way of taking in the pants without sewing. Here's the thing though. You will not get instructions with your kit. You will have a bunch of parts. There are the 'button' parts that will screw on to the 'screw' parts. There is a straight wire part and a curved wire part. There is also a screw driver.

You will punch a hole into the waist band of your jeans with the screw driver (or other sharp thing) and, using the button and screw, attach the curved piece to one section of your jeans at the waist band. Then the distance away you want to take up you attach the straight piece. Now you can move the straight piece over to the curve part and hook them together.

It is a good idea to measure things before you punch the holes unless you want to put a lot of holes into your clothes. These are easy to remove and the holes are not that big.

Not sure yet how well they wash but I am going to make sure they are unhooked before I try.

I just took up embroidery again after a long absence and needed to restock my floss. I thought this set would provide a good basic start. There is only one skein of each color. Each skein does have the DNC numbers on it The floss is decent quality with a bit of a sheen like the more expensive brands. It pulls apart well too and is fairly smooth in texture. I'm not doing anything fancy that will be judged by anyone, so I think it will work just fine.
In the set I got (I'm not sure if the packages vary) there are:
Five purples (209, 210, 550, 3837, 3746)
Nine pinks ( 602, 603,605, 967, 3609, 963, 3716, 3708, 3705)
Eight blues (162, 820, 823, 996, 3840, 3846, 3808, 3847)
Four orange/reds ( 326,740, 666, 608)
Eight tan/yellows (317, 445, 444, 742, 725, 435, 3771, 3861)
Plus one each of white, black, silver, and then two dk browns.
There is also included 9 (I was expecting 10) good plastic spools, 10 nice needles, and a small, cheap seam ripper with cap.
I do wish there were more red and orange floss, but that's just me. All in all a good start to getting back to relaxing embroidery time.

I reduced from 5 STARS to 4 STARS because the threaded end of the 8mm mandrel doesn't fit the handle. The 3 smaller mandrels fit perfectly. I can only assume it's because it was an already-opened item and the 8mm mandrel was probably originally in a set with a handle that it did fit. I've contacted the seller to inquire about replacing only the 8mm mandrel. If they can't or won't... oh well. Actually, a DIY wooden handle might last longer than the acrylic handle that's already cracked when I got it. I added another photo showing that the 8mm mandrel doesn't fit.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:
I haven't used it yet but I'm giving 5 Stars in good faith that it will meet my functional expectations. If it doesn't, I'll edit my review.

You can see in the photo that it came in the wrong packaging; HOWEVER, I DID GET EXACTLY WHAT I ORDERED (round jump ring maker) and for a fraction of the cost. The package for the oval jump ring maker had been opened and the round mandrels had been inserted to replace them in the package. I have no problem with that considering I paid less than $8 even after tax. I am not sure how much I will trust the acrylic not to break because I do see a couple of cracks where the nut has been put into it. To be on the safe side, I'll probably put some super glue around it and maybe some tape just to give the acrylic extra support and help it to last longer. Overall, it seems like it will do what I need it to do so I'll give 5 stars for now.

Side note: I wish I could get some larger mandrels and oval mandrels to go with it without buying multiple kits but that isn't what this review is about.

Very effective. I was skeptical, but it works very well. I had a lot of gunk on the sole of my iron, and it's gone now.

Be sure to use a HOT iron (not warm). Use Q-Tips to clean out steam holes in sole; 2x in each hole with a clean Q-Tip each time. Iron over a scrap of white fabric (or old dish towel) with iron on hot and the fabric wet (wring it out but it should be wet enough to produce a goodly amount of steam when ironed on), to check if anything lurks in the steam holes.

This stuff is like trying to mix slightly softened taffy. It's SO THICK!! It literally hurt my upper arms to mix for 3 mins. And you better be fast to pot this stuff cause it cured fast. If you dont have a vaccum chamber, you must have a pressure pot. You have to have one or the other to get rid of the bubbles. Both would be ideal because the pressure pot still didn't get rid of them all. Thankfully they weren't so bad they affected my dice.... looks like most of the bubbles were what got stuck on the surface. So do not buy thinking the fridge method will work. You cannot let this sit and hope it will degas on its own.
Other than that, its SUPER clear, and sturdy. I will get many dice out of the molds.
Recommendation: Mix, then immediately put in a vaccum chamber for 5 mins. Then immediately pour, then right into a pressure pot. Even pouring it, its so thick, makes more bubbles. But waaaaay less then pressure pot alone. And I had it up to 40 psi.

Will buy again...when I have a vaccum chamber.

I have had this die for a short time, but there is nothing about this die set I don't like! It cuts very nicely, with very clean edges. The leaves are quite nice and as with the flower, all 5 dies have embossing areas, as seen in the photo when enlarged. Conclusion: Wonderful die at a great price!

An absolute must for the Women's March.  The yarn is just the right color and price point to be able to make several hats.  I think I got about 2.5 from one skein, so I ended up making one hat striped.  The yarn is, as usual, a bit rough to work with as it is acrylic but for a major event where you are donating to charity and have no idea who is going to get it, you definitely want to use acrylic instead of wool because people can be allergic to wool.  Other people don't realize that you have to hand wash some wools or they don't go in the dryer or they're going to felt.  So this is a good choice when you don't know the recipient and you don't know what kind of care your work is going to receive.  The yarn is also easy care and this particular shade isn't quite as "shocking" as some of their other colorways!  I think "Pretty n' pink" is the most neon of their pinks.  Overall, it's a staple workhorse yarn.

I’m an artist and a teacher. This is excellent quality air dry clay.
There is a chemical odor and I am guessing by the profile that it is a non-toxic drying agent. The color clays have a minimal staining effect. As with most clay it can be messy, but it cleans up easily.
It is easy to texture compared to some other name brands that I have used. It doesn’t grab, pull, and separate much when texturing, as some of the other brands. While working on a piece, you can have a watered down bit of it set off to the side and add it to the surface of your piece as to work in texture, like a slip.
It dries well and I haven’t noticed any cracking.
I’m impressed with it and will be adding this brand to my go-to supply list!
Would recommend!

I received these paint brushes about a week ago and have been sampling each and every one of them these past days. Having been an artist for over 50 years, painting mostly with oils and encaustics, I decided I wanted to try dabbling in water colour so I got this set of brushes to do just that! I must say, this is really so different from painting with my other mediums, but I just MAY be getting the hang of it.

But this review is not about my painting, rather, it's for me to relate to you my experience with these brushes!

It arrived packaged in a plain white rectangular box. The sellers sent this very well packaged to protect it and in a shipping box that was just the right size for it. Upon receipt, I opened the white box and slid out the packaged brushes and white plastic folding palate. The palate is very handy and has three little holders in graduating sizes to hold the paintbrushes with bristles up if you're out painting. I personally keep my paintbrushes in cups according to what type of brush it is and for whatever medium I am using. But this will work great if you are out plein aire painting!

Paint brushes are made by attaching natural or synthetic bristles, in this case nylon, to a handle, these brushes have wooden handles, by clamping them on with a piece of metal called a ferule. The handles on these brushes are longer than I am used to but I think I'll have no problem getting used to them. The brush bristles are made of nylon and held securely in the silver colour ferules which in turn, are secured quite well to the wooden brush handles. These brushes are for beginners and students, but since I am just starting out with watercolour, I thought I would purchase this great looking 9-piece set to save money rather than buying some expensive watercolour brushes individually...at least until I know if I am even going to pursue this medium. But I must say, these brushes are so nice and hold their shape so well, I plan on using these for a very long time to come! I found no brush hairs in my paintings as I worked, so that's a definite plus also!

The paint brush is an essential and iconic tool of any artist. Painting is a truly ancient method of creating, reproducing, and sharing artistic ideas and expressions that are even more varied and innumerable than artists themselves. It takes a truly versatile tool to explore the breadth of potential within the art of painting; through the millennia, paint brushes have proven more than equal to the task!

I would definitely recommend these brushes to friends and family if they are in need of a deluxe set of beginner brushes! The manufacturer did not skimp when producing these brushes! They are definitely a great buy!

I hope you found this review enjoyable, informative and have gotten something out of it to help you decide whether or not to make this purchase. If you have any questions about my experiences with this product, please leave me a comment and I will be happy to follow up on my review to be of assistance to you.

These powder have a wonderful glow. Want to mention, I'm not an artist and have never used these pigment powders before. When I did a little web research some medium suggestions were given. I tried a clear latex type paint and because these are more like sand and don't dissolve, didn't have much luck there. I did not want to use epoxy. Glue seemed to work the best for my purposes. I tried it by sprinkling the powders with a stencil. But you have to work kind of fast and it was not best.  If it dried in areas, I had to reapply. Also mix with an amount of glue and spread, this did not come out even. Not recommended for: my project was to create glow in the dark pavers and again I tried different applications. Each paver was even "sealed" with clear spray paint which I thought would hold. I mean spray seems to never come off when someone tags a wall! Unfortunately, It rained a few days and all of my hard work just disappeared. Literally almost every paver (12 of them) design had washed away!! :-( Or left very little remnants of what was previously there. So moral of the story, not for outside use. Although, if you're using epoxy; that may work. Also, to be fair, could be my lack of experience. They were all really cute at first. I mean not even a damn season out of my hard work. Disappointing. Another thing about outside, they faded rather fast as well. But again, great glow and would assume if you're using for an inside project and find the right medium and the room is dark enough; it's great. My pavers were pretty much under a street light, so that didn't help with seeing the glow. However, when inside under dark conditions; everything glowed beautifully.
